setup_task.sh tag_map and seed_library.py ML_PAPERS list; papers are inserted via seed_library.py --mode all which includes both CLASSIC_PAPERS and ML_PAPERS

pi_items.verified_value: LeCun, Y., Bengio, Y., and Hinton, G. (2015). Deep learning. Nature, 521(7553), 436-444. DOI: 10.1038/nature14539
pi_items.source: Web search of PubMed and Nature journal; confirmed as foundational deep learning review paper published May 28, 2015

pi_items.verified_value: Goodfellow, I.J., Pouget-Abadie, J., Mirza, M., et al. (2014). Generative Adversarial Nets. Proceedings of the 27th International Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems, Volume 2, pages 2672-2680
pi_items.source: Web search of arXiv, ACM Digital Library, and NIPS/NeurIPS archive; confirmed publication in NIPS 2014

pi_items.verified_value: Krizhevsky, A., Sutskever, I., and Hinton, G.E. (2012). ImageNet Classification with Deep Convolutional Neural Networks. Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems, 25, pages 1097-1105
pi_items.source: Web search of NIPS/NeurIPS proceedings and IEEE Xplore; confirmed as landmark AlexNet paper achieving 15.3% top-5 error on ILSVRC-2012

pi_items.verified_value: He, K., Zhang, X., Ren, S., and Sun, J. (2016). Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition. Proceedings of the IEEE Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition, Las Vegas, pages 770-778
pi_items.source: Web search of IEEE Xplore, Computer Vision Foundation, and arXiv; confirmed as ResNet paper winning ILSVRC 2015 classification with 3.57% error

privileged_info_summary: The task 'migrate_tags_to_collections@1' uses real papers seeded into a Zotero library database. Papers are from the ML_PAPERS list in seed_library.py: (1) 'Deep Learning' by LeCun et al., Nature 2015, vol. 521, pp. 436-444, DOI 10.1038/nature14539; (2) 'Generative Adversarial Nets' by Goodfellow et al., NIPS 2014, vol. 27; (3) 'ImageNet Classification with Deep Convolutional Neural Networks' by Krizhevsky et al., NIPS 2012, vol. 25, pp. 1097-1105; (4) 'Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition' by He et al., CVPR 2016, pp. 770-778. Papers are paired with tags 'dataset-mnist' and 'dataset-imagenet' as specified in setup_task.sh. The data is synthetic (programmatically inserted into Zotero) but based on real, published papers.
